preamble: The shift from [ĩ] to [ui] is a complex diphthongization that involves both a change in the nucleus and the development of a diphthongal glide.

examples.note: In some dialectal variants, nasal vowels show significant backing or rounding shifts resulting in [ui].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
